title: Repair lock-verifier reproducibility check (blank-temp -> seeded committed lock)
objective: Correct the byte-identical lock verifiers so --check tests REPRODUCIBILITY of the COMMITTED lock (its exact pins preserved) instead of performing a fresh latest-version resolution. Fix: in lock_tools.sh and lock_requirements.sh --check mode, SEED the temp output file with the committed lock (cp OUT_FILE TMP) BEFORE running the identical NON-upgrade uv pip compile, so uv uses the committed pins as existing-output preferences; then diff. No --upgrade, no re-lock of any dependency, no certifi upgrade. MUST NOT modify dependency_age_gate.py or weaken the 7-day (604800s) age gate. Preserve every security property: exact pins+hashes retained; separate production (requirements.txt) and tooling (requirements-tools.lock) locks; --check still FAILS on genuine manifest<->lock drift (a new/removed/changed .in requirement the committed lock does not satisfy); still FAILS on tampered or missing hashes; no silent dependency updates. Add regression tests under services/api/scripts/tests/: (1) a newer upstream transitive release does NOT break a PR whose committed lock still satisfies its .in inputs; (2) real manifest/lock drift still fails; (3) tampered/missing hashes still fail; (4) an intentional dependency addition/upgrade still invokes the age gate. Optionally the upstream-newer-version DISCOVERY may remain as a SEPARATE scheduled/reporting check but must not masquerade as committed-lock reproducibility nor block unrelated PRs. Freeze SHA; full CI; G5 dependency-security + G3 + G4 review of the changed workflow; return evidence before merge. Does NOT touch M4-T002 or any lock file's contents.
business_reason: Upstream certifi 2026.7.22 (released 2026-07-22) makes api-tooling-lock-verify fail on every unrelated PR (repo-wide deadlock; observed on PR #79) even though the committed tooling lock still satisfies requirements-tools.in. Root cause: lock_tools.sh --check (and its mirror lock_requirements.sh --check) compile into a BLANK mktemp file, so uv resolves to LATEST instead of using the committed lock as existing-output version preferences. This is a design defect in the verifier, not a real lock/manifest drift. The same latent bug in lock_requirements.sh will break production-lock CI the moment any runtime transitive dep releases. Owner directive 2026-07-22: repair the verifier, do NOT re-lock or upgrade certifi, do NOT weaken the 7-day age gate.
inputs: services/api/scripts/dependency_age_gate.py (age gate — READ-ONLY, must not change behavior)
requirement_refs: docs/LOW_STORAGE_CLOUD_DEVELOPMENT_POLICY.md (heavy tests run on CI, not locally)
forbidden_paths: any M4-T002 / M4-T003 path or worktree
acceptance_scenarios: AS-1 (primary): committed tooling lock unchanged + no upstream release relevant -> lock_tools.sh --check PASSES (byte-identical after seeding).

AS-2 (repo-wide-deadlock regression): a newer upstream transitive version exists upstream (simulated) but the committed lock still satisfies requirements-tools.in -> --check PASSES (does NOT pull the newer version; unrelated PRs are not blocked).
AS-3 (genuine drift, failure): a requirement in the .in manifest is added/removed/changed so the committed lock no longer satisfies it -> --check FAILS with a clear diff.
AS-4 (tamper, failure): a hash in the committed lock is altered or removed -> --check FAILS.

AS-5 (age-gate preserved): an intentional dependency addition/upgrade still routes through dependency_age_gate.py and a <7-day (604799s) package still FAILS the age gate; the gate's behavior is unchanged (byte-identical dependency_age_gate.py).
AS-6 (boundary): both lock_tools.sh AND lock_requirements.sh receive the identical fix; production-lock verifier no longer performs latest-version resolution.

progress_log.message: G0 PASS; claimed; root cause captured in M0-T021-diagnosis.md
